steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Recall the property of similar figures
steps.content: When two figures are similar, their corresponding angles are equal. This is true regardless of the scale factor between them. The problem states that quadrilateral $P'Q'R'S'$ is similar to quadrilateral $PQRS$, with $P$ corresponding to $P'$, $Q$ to $Q'$, $R$ to $R'$, and $S$ to $S'$.

steps.title: Apply correspondence to find angle P'
steps.content:
Since $P$ and $P'$ are corresponding angles in similar figures, they must be equal.

We are given that the measure of angle $P$ is $30^{\circ}$. Therefore, the measure of angle $P'$ is also $30^{\circ}$. The scale factor of $3$ (side lengths of $P'Q'R'S'$ are $3$ times those of $PQRS$) affects only the side lengths, not the angles.

That is the correct answer.
